title: SPR strategy zones — 5 bands by stack-to-pot ratio (rake-agnostic, all stack depths)
bodyMarkdown:
Stack-to-pot ratio (SPR) at the flop = effective stack / pot. The five canonical zones drive postflop strategy.

- MICRO (SPR 0–2): commit-or-fold board. Top pair good kicker is usually committing.
- LOW (SPR 2–4): TPTK / overpair commits; medium pairs face 2-street decisions.
- MEDIUM (SPR 4–8): standard postflop. Range advantage drives sizing; cbet ranges are wider.
- HIGH (SPR 8–13): implied-odds territory. Sets, suited connectors, and gap-1 hands gain value; one-pair hands lose stack-off threshold.
- DEEP (SPR 13+): deep-stack play. Set-mining + nut hands; one-pair retreats; sizing tells more than range vs range advantage.

Derivation: SPR = effectiveStack / pot at the moment of measurement (typically flop). Zones reflect the geometry of how many pot-sized bets fit before all-in. Rake-agnostic; field-invariant across cash and tournament. See POKER_THEORY.md §3 (Postflop Theory) for the zone-by-zone strategy implications.
theoryCitation: POKER_THEORY.md §3 (Postflop Theory) — SPR zone strategy. Phase 1 5-zone enumeration ratified at project_rake_spr_antes (2026-03-29).

assumptions.field: all 9-handed live cash and tournament fields; formula is field-invariant

atomicityJustification: Single derivation (5 SPR zones with strategy implications) presented as one card.
